denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION
 Date of Notice: 03/18/2026
Cigna Open Access Plus (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W102328186          Group #: GRP-1294
Authorization / Reference #: UM-2026-144299
Claim #: CLM-2026-203583
Date of Service (requested): 02/26/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Central States Medical Associates
 Dear Member,
 This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination. We received your provider's precertification / medical necessity review request on 02/28/2026 for the requested service.: High-volume lumbar puncture trial
Diagnosis (as submitted): [REDACTED]
Denial code: CO-96 — Non-covered charge — service denied as not medically necessary under plan criteria. Clinical policy applied: Coverage Policy 114 under Cigna Medical Coverage Policy (CPG) and Milliman Care Guidelines (MCG)
 EXPLANATION OF DECISION
Our review applied the criteria in Coverage Policy 114 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Cigna medical policy requires documented failure of prerequisite therapies before High-volume lumbar puncture trial. Submitted records do not establish failure of required lower-cost options for Normal pressure hydrocephalus, suspected (G91.2). Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with step therapy missing. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval. Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, including plain-language descriptions, as required by applicable federal standards. If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials. YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file and the clinical criteria we used in this determination. Submit your request to the address on this notice or through your plan's member portal. Your treating physician may request a peer-to-peer discussion with a Cigna medical director regarding this determination. APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination. If this denial is upheld after internal review, you may have the right to request an independent external review of our decision at no cost to you, subject to applicable federal and state requirements. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W102328186) and reference UM-2026-144299. You may also have the right to receive, upon request and without charge, copies of documents relevant to this determination. Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above. Sincerely,
 Cigna Health Care Management
Medical Review Unit
clinical_context: The 42-year-old member has Normal pressure hydrocephalus, suspected (G91.2). She has been treated under commercial coverage with High-volume lumbar puncture trial recommended as the next appropriate step. Gait apraxia, incontinence, cognitive slowing; Evans index >0.3. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that step therapy missing bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings that were submitted with the original request submitted in Q1 2026 but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if her care is delayed further.

insurer_voice.reasoning: Letter reads as authentic Cigna correspondence with MCG Care Guidelines and procedural tone.
